<|11.06|> And let's review a bit of context to remind us how we got here.<|15.20|>
<|16.54|> Jesus set his face to go to Jerusalem in Luke 9, and finally arrives there ten chapters later in Luke 9.<|26.40|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And in these two chapters since his arrival, a lot has happened.<|5.78|>
<|6.78|> He weeps over Jerusalem.<|8.08|>
<|8.70|> He cleanses the temple.<|9.98|>
<|10.48|> And he teaches in the temple.<|12.58|>
<|13.16|> While the chief priests, scribes, and leaders of the people look for a way to discredit and destroy him.<|21.30|>

<|5.46|> First, tell us by what authority do you do these things?<|11.04|>
<|11.76|> Who is giving you authority?<|13.92|>
<|15.22|> Second, is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar or not?<|22.46|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And finally, in our gospel lesson for today, the Sadducees ask,<|5.14|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> All three are attempts to ensnare Jesus to ultimately compromise his authority.<|8.74|>
<|10.44|> There are so many things to tease out and reflect upon in this gospel reading.<|18.30|>
